structural_analysis.missing_signal_hypothesis: The agent never receives a terminal reward because the required landing events (safe_contact, stable_landing) are never triggered. All dense shaping rewards (approach_region, stability, landing_quality) allow the agent to accumulate high total reward without ever landing. A progressive, intermediate signal that explicitly moves the agent closer to the landing pad is needed to bridge the gap between hovering near the pad and actually touching down.
structural_analysis.why_local_edit_is_insufficient: Previous local edits (decreasing r_approach_region weight, increasing r_stable_landing_once weight, relaxing landing duration) all failed to induce a single terminal payment. The terminal events remain unreachably sparse; only a new process signal focused on the tighter landing region can provide refined guidance.
memory_constraints: Do not repeat weight adjustments that have already failed
Do not add unprotected repeatable event bonuses
safety_constraints: Use only available task metrics and operators
Ensure the new component does not create a dominant shaping loop

reward_editor.rationale: Add a metric_delta component on 'landing_region_score' (the tighter pad-region metric) to reward incremental movement toward the landing pad. This provides a dense, intermediate process signal that complements existing shaping without creating exploit risks. It is a generic, non-environment-specific signal and uses only available metrics. The component uses positive_only=true to avoid penalizing temporary regressions, and weight is moderate to avoid overshadowing terminal incentives.

diagnosis: Terminal landing event never triggered despite terminal reward rebalancing. Adding a metric_delta on landing_region_score creates a bridge signal to encourage approach to the precise landing zone.
